title: Rhodanthemum mesatlanticum
uses: Rhodanthemum mesatlanticum is a plant native to the Canary Islands, and research has shown that it may have potential medicinal uses, particularly in the area of antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Studies have suggested that extracts from the plant may have beneficial effects on cardiovascular health and may help to protect against cell damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, Rhodanthemum mesatlanticum has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications. Further studies are necessary to confirm the efficacy and safety of Rhodanthemum mesatlanticum for medicinal use.
taxonomy: Rhodanthemum mesatlanticum belongs to the family Asteraceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is a type of daisy, characterized by its yellow or white flowers and silvery-gray foliage. Within the Asteraceae family, Rhodanthemum mesatlanticum is part of the tribe Anthemideae. The Anthemideae tribe includes various species of daisies and related plants. Rhodanthemum mesatlanticum is native to the Iberian Peninsula and the western coast of Africa.
